assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE upgrades ~20 nodes simultaneously regardless of maxSurge settings' and explains this is the bottleneck

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: Response states '150+ upgrade batches minimum' for 3,000 nodes and calculates 'Total duration estimate: 25-40 hours minimum (well beyond your 8-hour window)'

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: Response provides a detailed 'multi-weekend strategy' with Phase 1 (Weekend 1) for CPU pools and Phase 2 (Weekend 2-3) for GPU pools

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Phase 1: CPU pools (Weekend 1)' first, stating 'These typically upgrade faster' and includes an anti-pattern warning 'Don't upgrade GPU and CPU pools simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Long-running training protection' section with coordinated upgrade approach: 'Checkpoint active training jobs', 'Cordon training node pool', and 'Restart training from checkpoints'

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Extended maintenance windows' section with specific commands to expand windows and mentions 'Both weekend days' and '20-hour window (Friday 6pm - Saturday 2pm)'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: Response does not mention cluster autoscaler limitations or how it interacts with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: Response does not mention node limits, the 15,000 standard limit, or the increased 65,000 limit for AI workloads in GKE 1.31+
